_about: MCP server configuration template — drop-in starting point for:
- Claude Code: a project-scoped .mcp.json at the repo root (recommended,
commit-safe if it contains only ${VAR} placeholders), or user scope via
Format note: JSON does not allow comments. Per-server prose is stored in
_comments arrays, and Claude Code / your runtime will ignore unknown
underscore-prefixed keys. If you prefer real comments, rename the file
to .jsonc and ship both — the stripped .json for runtimes that demand it,
the .jsonc for humans reading the source of truth.
Secrets: use ${ENV_VAR} syntax. Claude Code expands these at launch from
the launching shell's env. Never check in real secrets.
Hosted servers use {"type": "http", "url": ...} and authenticate via
OAuth on first connect — no token in the file at all.
Pattern: comment out servers you don't need rather than deleting them — the
next person to onboard will thank you.

_comments: GitHub's official hosted MCP server. Repo browsing, PR creation, issue
management, releases. OAuth handled by the host on first connect — no
token needed in this file. Self-hosted alternative: the Go binary at
github/github-mcp-server (Docker: ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server,
env GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN). The old npm package
@modelcontextprotocol/server-github is archived/deprecated.
Read/write within an allow-listed root. Always scope to the narrowest
directory possible; the agent CAN write here.
Multiple roots: pass each as a positional arg after the package name.
Postgres MCP Pro (crystaldba/postgres-mcp) — maintained replacement for
the archived @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres reference server.
--access-mode=restricted enforces read-only with query safeguards;
use a read replica connection string in prod. If you need writes, add a
separate entry with --access-mode=unrestricted and have your AGENTS.md
scope which agents may call it.
kubernetes-mcp-server (GitHub: containers/kubernetes-mcp-server).
Reads the standard KUBECONFIG env var for cluster/context selection.
--read-only restricts to read verbs; if you drop it and allow
apply/delete, scope by RBAC on the service account that owns the
KUBECONFIG, NOT by trusting the agent. Cluster admin via agent = bad
day waiting to happen.
HashiCorp's official terraform-mcp-server — a Go binary distributed
via Docker and GitHub releases (NOT npm; the npm package of the same
name is an unrelated community project). Provides Terraform Registry
provider/module doc lookup and Terraform-aware assistance.
Browser automation via Playwright (Chromium/Firefox/WebKit) using
accessibility-tree interaction. Useful for visual regression,
screenshots, and scraping JS-rendered pages. Replaces the archived
@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer. Sandbox carefully — the agent
can navigate anywhere.
slack-mcp-server (community, maintained) — the old
@modelcontextprotocol/server-slack reference server is archived.
Post messages, read channel history, search. Supports xoxp (user) or
xoxb (bot) tokens; see the package README for scope guidance.
Read and write Notion pages, databases, and comments via the official
@notionhq/notion-mcp-server. Integration token needs to be invited to
each database/page individually (Notion's permission model is
per-resource, not workspace-wide). Hosted alternative with OAuth:
{"type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.notion.com/mcp"}.
Issue tracking. Create/update issues, comment, assign, transition state.
Linear's MCP server is hosted-only (no stdio npm package); OAuth on
first connect.
Jira + Confluence (Atlassian's hosted server). Search, read, comment,
transition. Cloud only; OAuth on first connect.
Library documentation lookup. Searches a corpus of vendor docs
indexed by package + version. Massively reduces hallucinated API usage.
Free tier is generous; no key needed for low-volume use.
Read issues, events, releases, and project stats from Sentry.
Auth via SENTRY_ACCESS_TOKEN (a user auth token). Hosted alternative
with OAuth: {"type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp"}.
Stripe's official @stripe/mcp. Select tool surface with --tools=
(comma-separated, or 'all'). USE A RESTRICTED KEY scoped to read-only
resources for agent use; the agent should never see a full secret key.
Hosted alternative with OAuth: {"type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.stripe.com"}.
Web search via the Brave Search API (official @brave package — the old
@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search is archived). Useful for
current-events lookup and library/vendor doc discovery when context7
misses. Rate limits apply per the Brave plan you're on.
Veriswarm — agent governance: trust scoring, PII guard, audit ledger. Free tier available.
65+ tools spanning real-time trust scoring, tool-call interception,
portable JWT credentials, and a hash-chained audit ledger.
Cross-framework. One option among several for governance layers; see the
examples/code-review and examples/devops-automation AGENTS.md for the
hook points where this typically fits.
Persistent knowledge graph across sessions. Good for long-running
projects with carry-over context (curriculum generation, big refactors).
Storage path is set via the MEMORY_FILE_PATH env var (a JSONL file);
keep it on durable storage, not /tmp.
dbt Labs' official dbt-mcp (PyPI). dbt CLI + semantic layer + discovery
tools. Honors DBT_PROFILES_DIR / DBT_PROJECT_DIR.
Pair with the postgres or snowflake entry for the warehouse layer.
Snowflake Labs' official snowflake-labs-mcp (PyPI). Cortex + object
management + SQL tools, configured via a service config YAML.
Prefer key-pair or PAT auth over passwords. Scope the role to
read-only for agent use; create a separate writable entry if you
need INSERT/UPDATE.
